scope.summary: Implement washed denim preset variants (stonewashed, acid-washed, bleach-washed) as seeded tailor_material_presets forks of the base denim preset with calibrated compliance, physics, and PBR albedo color diffs to match MD 2025.1 washed denim parity
acceptance_criteria: Three additional is_system_preset=true rows MUST be seeded: slug='denim-stonewashed', slug='denim-acid-washed', slug='denim-bleach-washed', each forked from the base 'denim' preset row
Stonewashed variant MUST have slightly higher compliance (softer) than base denim (stretch_weft approx 1e-7 vs 5e-8) and lower buckling_stiffness (more worn, drapes more softly)

Acid washed and bleach washed variants MUST differ in their physics_json internal_damping and friction from base denim to reflect the different finishing treatments (acid wash: reduced friction; bleach wash: stiffer, closer to base)

Each washed denim preset MUST carry a display_color hint (RGB) in an optional metadata_json column or equivalent so the UvTexture group can generate a correct base albedo tone without a separate texture asset
All three variants MUST pass PRESET_NO_NAN, PRESET_STRETCH_NONZERO, and PRESET_DENSITY_POS drape validation
The tailor_fabric_preset_fork MCP tool MUST be the canonical mechanism for creating user-authored washed denim variants beyond the three seeded ones; the seeded rows demonstrate the fork pattern
ModelManual MUST list the three washed denim slugs as available preset archetypes so a model authoring a washed denim garment can select the correct archetype without inventing values
scope.risk_if_missed: MD 2025.1 washed denim presets are a visible production feature used by fashion design workflows to distinguish garment handle without manual parameter tuning. Without them the Tailor preset library under-delivers for denim-heavy workflows, and models authoring denim garments will default to base denim compliance for all denim types, producing physically incorrect drape for stonewashed vs raw denim. This is a user-visible parity gap against MD 2025.1 in a high-frequency production material.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
